perturbation.original_text: Watchit will provide the content on BetaSp format and reserves the right to modify the content to reflect sponsorship by an advertiser and advertisers. Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to not edit or modify the above content.
perturbation.changed_text: Watchit will provide the content on BetaSp format and reserves the right to modify the content to reflect sponsorship by an advertiser and advertisers. Oceanic Time Warner Cable has the right to make minor edits to the content for technical reasons, but will endeavor to maintain the original intent. However, Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to not edit or modify the above content for promotional purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding Oceanic Time Warner Cable's editing rights. The original text clearly states that Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to not edit or modify the above content. The added text now grants Oceanic Time Warner Cable the right to make minor edits, but restricts this to 'technical reasons'. The contradiction arises because it is unclear what constitutes a 'minor edit' or 'technical reason' vs. 'promotional purposes'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the earlier statement stating that Oceanic Time Warner Cable has no such right, possibly leading to disputes over what editing is permissible. It creates a contradiction in this part of the agreement: Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to not edit or modify the above content.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Watchit will provide the content on BetaSp format and reserves the right to modify the content to reflect sponsorship by an advertiser and advertisers. Oceanic Time Warner Cable has the right to make minor edits to the content for technical reasons, but will endeavor to maintain the original intent. However, Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to not edit or modify the above content for promotional purposes. Oceanic Time Warner Cable will use the content solely on channel 777, the Las Vegas channel.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees not to edit or modify the content. The changed text grants them the right to make 'minor edits for technical reasons,' while simultaneously maintaining the agreement not to edit for 'promotional purposes.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the definition of 'minor edit for technical reasons' is subjective and can lead to disputes, directly conflicting with the earlier restriction against any editing, as per the provided definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with data on viewership to both Channel 777 and specifically to the content provided by Watchit.
perturbation.changed_text: Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with data on viewership to both Channel 777 and specifically to the content provided by Watchit. Ocean Time Warner Cable will only provide general viewership data and not specific demographics unless they deem its release reasonable.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity in the data sharing agreement. The original text stipulates that Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide viewership data. The addition contradicts this by stating that the provision of specific demographics is now conditional and depends on Ocean Time Warner Cable's discretion. This creates an in-text contradiction: Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with data on viewership to both Channel 777 and specifically to the content provided by Watchit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with data on viewership to both Channel 777 and specifically to the content provided by Watchit. Ocean Time Warner Cable will only provide general viewership data and not specific demographics unless they deem its release reasonable. Ocean Time Warner Cable will be able to not include any content that it deems inappropriate or distasteful.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide viewership data. The changed text adds a condition that the provision of specific demographics depends on Ocean Time Warner Cable's discretion. This directly contradicts the initial statement that viewership data, implicitly including demographic information, will be provided, thus creating an in-text contradiction regarding the scope and obligation of data provision, especially in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement will be in effect until the end of 2006 and will be evaluated at that time. Both parties agree to discuss use of information gathered form this arrangement before using it in any kind of promotional or public message.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement will be in effect until the end of 2006 and will be evaluated at that time. Both parties agree to discuss the potential use of information gathered from this arrangement. Either party may use the information for promotional or public messaging with a good faith effort to acknowledge the other party's contribution.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the condition for using information gathered from the arrangement. Originally, both parties agreed to *discuss* the *use of* information *before* its deployment in any promotional or public message. The modified text states that both parties agree to discuss the *potential* use, and either party *may* use the information *with a good faith effort to acknowledge the other party's contribution*. The original wording suggests a collaborative decision-making process, whereas the changed wording permits unilateral use provided good faith acknowledgment is given. This contradiction could create conflict if one party uses the data without the other's consent. The term contradicting is: Both parties agree to discuss use of information gathered form this arrangement before using it in any kind of promotional or public message.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement will be in effect until the end of 2006 and will be evaluated at that time. Both parties agree to discuss the potential use of information gathered from this arrangement. Either party may use the information for promotional or public messaging with a good faith effort to acknowledge the other party's contribution. Accepted by: Oceanic Time Warner Cable/Date Watchit Media/Date Source: WATCHIT MEDIA, INC., 8-K, 12/1/2006
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction because it alters a previously agreed-upon condition for using information gathered from the arrangement. Originally, both parties agreed to *discuss* the *use of* information *before* its deployment. The modified text states that both parties agree to discuss the *potential* use, and either party *may* use the information *with a good faith effort to acknowledge the other party's contribution*. The original wording suggests a collaborative decision-making process, whereas the changed wording permits unilateral use provided good faith acknowledgment is given. This contradicts the initial agreement, potentially leading to conflict if one party uses the data without the other's prior consent or agreement.
